marketState: bullish-leaning but low-conviction — favorable seasonality/weather offset by a storage surplus and a front price already slightly rich to fair value
summary: NG front trades $3.274, about 4.2pp above its seasonal median path (-3.9% YTD vs -8.1% median) and inside the fair-value band ($2.429-$3.429, fair $2.929), but 'SLIGHTLY OVER' fair at every horizon out to plus21D. Seasonality odds tilt higher with time (56/50/56/63% up at 1/2/3/4 weeks, +2.3% typical 4-week move) though reliability is explicitly flagged Low this week (51pp spread). Weather demand is running well above the 5-year normal in both the 1-7D (+15%, rangePos 0.93) and 8-16D (+20.6%, rangePos 0.84) windows and still climbing (+25.7 in 24h) — the strongest single input — but with rangePos already near the top of its own range there is limited room for further upside surprise. Managed money is net short (-64,808 contracts) yet only at the 31st percentile of the past 5 years, so the short side isn't crowded. Working against the bulls: storage is +175 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish, bigger-than-forecast weekly build, the near-dated futures curve is in backwardation (Sep/Oct priced below the August front), implying the market already expects some of today's heat premium to fade as contracts roll, and daily/weekly technicals carry a bearish 50<200 EMA cross even as 1h/4h momentum is bullish. Net: the top-priority seasonality/weather/fair-value inputs favor a cautious long bias, but storage, curve roll-down and higher-timeframe technicals argue against chasing strength, so conviction is kept low with entries preferring pullbacks over the current print.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality favors higher prices with time (63% up at 4 weeks) and price already sits above its seasonal median track, while weather demand is well above 5-year normal into the 8-16 day window and fair value itself is rising ($2.929 now to $3.067 by plus21D). This is tempered by a +175 Bcf storage surplus with a bearish build surprise and a bearish daily/weekly EMA structure, plus the Sep/Oct futures being priced below the August front (roll-down risk), so the trade is sized small and entries favor pullbacks rather than the current premium.
longTerm.invalidation: A daily/weekly close below $2.80, a widening storage surplus over consecutive reports, or 1-7D/8-16D weather demand fading back toward the 5-year normal would invalidate this thesis.
